name: Kazakh Khanate
note: Emerged from the Uzbek (Shaybanid) breakup when Janybek and Giray led a nomadic confederation into the Kazakh steppe. The three zhuz (hordes: Great, Middle, Small) structure defined Kazakh society. Gradually subordinated by Russian expansion; the Small Zhuz accepted Russian protectorate in 1731 under Dzungar pressure.

figures.name: Janybek Khan
figures.significance: Co-founder of the Kazakh Khanate with Giray; established Kazakh identity as distinct from Uzbeks

figures.name: Ablai Khan
figures.significance: Greatest 18th-century Kazakh ruler; united the zhuz against the Dzungars; played Russia and China against each other
